description_official: DRAFT — verify against current Navy Enlisted Classification Manual. Boatswain's Mates are the deck-side foundation of the Navy — ship handling, line handling, anchoring, small-boat operations, and underway replenishment.
description_plain: If a ship is a city, the BM is the infrastructure crew. You run deck evolutions: underway replenishments, anchor detail, small-boat ops, line handling during pier work. Physical job, lots of time outdoors, and you earn surface warfare qualifications as you advance. Traditional rating with a long history in the fleet.
description_tldr: U.S. Navy deck seaman — runs shipboard deck operations including underway replenishment, anchoring, and small-boat handling.

asvab.notes: Navy combines ASVAB line scores; BM typically requires a lower composite than electronics/nuclear ratings.

physical_requirements.details: Standard Navy physical requirements; deck work is physical.

training_pipeline.location: Great Lakes, IL (A-School) or assigned ship
training_pipeline.description: BM is one of several ratings that historically accepted direct-to-fleet assignments — verify current pipeline.

compensation.typical_entry_rank: E-1 through E-3 depending on advancement programs

special_pay.amount: Varies by rank and months at sea
special_pay.conditions: When assigned to a sea-duty ship

transferable_certifications: Merchant Mariner Credential (MMC) endorsements via sea time credit

typical_billets: Deck watch stander, line handler, small boat crew

standard_progression.notes: Rating strike: becomes 'BM3' once rating is earned.

typical_billets: Leading Petty Officer of a deck division

standard_progression.notes: Making Chief is a major career milestone in the Navy.

typical_billets: Senior Chief billets at the department or ship level
Command Master Chief of a ship or shore command

b_billets.title: Recruiter
b_billets.eligibility: E-5 to E-7 with recruiting duty assignment.

b_billets.title: Recruit Division Commander (RDC)
b_billets.description: Equivalent of Marine DI — trains Navy recruits at RTC Great Lakes.
b_billets.eligibility: E-5+, strong record, RDC School graduate.

lateral_moves.title: Operations Specialist
lateral_moves.description: Radar and combat information center rating — common lateral for BMs who want more technical work.

lateral_moves.title: Quartermaster
lateral_moves.description: Navigation rating; related to deck and bridge work.

reclass_options.title: Conversion (rating change)
reclass_options.description: Navy allows conversion between ratings at re-enlistment or through specific programs.
reclass_options.eligibility: Target rating ASVAB, school seat availability.

career_path.career_capstone_notes: Career BMs can make Command Master Chief, Fleet Master Chief, or MCPON (senior enlisted of the Navy). Sea-shore rotation is critical to career planning — verify current OPNAVINST 1301.

sources.name: Navy COOL and MyNavy HR (DRAFT references)

notes: DRAFT — verify current A-School structure, sea-shore rotation, and MMC credit policy.

description_official: DRAFT — verify. Hospital Corpsmen provide medical care in Navy hospitals, clinics, and at sea, and serve as the sole medical provider for Marine Corps ground units (the Fleet Marine Force).
description_plain: Navy medics. Dual-track rating — some HMs stay in hospitals and clinics, others attach to the Marine Corps (FMF) and deploy with infantry battalions. The FMF side is closer to combat medicine than any other non-Army pathway. NECs layer specialties on top: advanced trauma, dental, behavioral health, SARC (SEAL medic), and dozens more.
description_tldr: Navy medical rating — hospital and clinic care, plus the Marine Corps FMF combat-medic pipeline. One of the most specialty-diverse ratings in the military.

physical_requirements.details: Standard Navy; FMF-track requires Marine-level PFT standards.

training_pipeline.location: Camp Pendleton, CA or Camp Lejeune, NC
training_pipeline.description: Combat medic training for corpsmen assigned to Marine units.

salary_range_usd.notes: Requires RN bridge program; FMF corpsmen often qualify for accelerated paths.
IPAP is a path to commission as a PA officer; civilian transition is lucrative.

daily_life: In a hospital or clinic: shifts on wards, patient intake, medication administration, or specialty rotations. At sea on a ship: run the sick bay, conduct sea-duty physicals, respond to onboard injuries. With a Marine unit (FMF): train with the infantry, deploy attached to a company or battalion, and be the medical provider on patrols. FMF corpsmen live the Marine Corps lifestyle more than Navy — field exercises, rucks, and combat medicine training.

faq.question: Hospital corpsman vs. FMF corpsman — can I pick?
faq.answer: You can indicate preference. Some corpsmen start hospital-side and request FMTB later; others ship straight to FMTB. Not everyone gets FMF duty — it depends on orders and needs of the Navy.

faq.question: Will I be a 'Doc' to Marines?
faq.answer: Yes. Corpsmen attached to Marine units are called 'Doc' regardless of their actual rank. Deep tradition between Marines and their corpsmen.

faq.question: Can I become a civilian nurse or PA?
faq.answer: Yes — HM is one of the strongest civilian-transition ratings. NREMT is often earned during training. RN bridge programs accept HM service credit. IPAP lets you commission as a PA officer in-service.

notable_holders.name: Donald E. Ballard
notable_holders.notable_for: Medal of Honor recipient for actions as a Navy corpsman in Vietnam.

notable_holders.name: Richard D. Dewert
notable_holders.notable_for: Medal of Honor recipient (posthumous) for actions during the Korean War.

promotion_speed.fast_promote_notes: NECs (specialties) like SARC, SEAL medic, IDC, and advanced dental accelerate promotion. Navy-Wide Advancement Exam performance matters heavily.
promotion_speed.slow_promote_notes: HM is a large rating; some NEC pools are oversubscribed.
promotion_speed.cutting_score_note: Navy uses Final Multiple Score (FMS) combining exam score, performance evaluations, awards, and time-in-rate. Cutoffs vary by rating and NEC.

notes: DRAFT — verify current NECs, FMTB course length, and civilian reciprocity for EMT/RN programs.
